$$ 10 \text{ miles/day} \times 5 \text{ days/week} = 50 \text{ miles/week} $$

The fuel consumption for 50 miles is:
$$ \frac{50 \text{ miles}}{10 \text{ miles/gallon}} = 5 \text{ gallons} $$

The cost of 5 gallons of gas at $3 per gallon is:
$$ 5 \text{ gallons} \times 3 \text{ dollars/gallon} = 15 \text{ dollars} $$

To determine how many inches long Milo can make his mosaic, we need to calculate the total number of glass chips he has and then determine how many square inches he can cover with those chips.

1. Milo has 2 bags of glass chips, and each bag contains 72 chips:
   $$
   2 \times 72 = 144 \text{ chips}
   $$

2. Each square inch of the mosaic requires 12 glass chips. So, the number of square inches that can be covered with 144 chips is:
   $$
   \frac{144}{12} = 12 \text{ square inches}
   $$

3. The mosaic is 3 inches tall, so the length of the mosaic in inches is:
   $$
   \frac{12 \text{ square inches}}{3 \text{ inches (height)}} = 4 \text{ inches}
   $$

Final answer: 4

$$
\text{Time} = \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Speed}} = \frac{30 \, \text{miles}}{6 \, \text{mph}} = 5 \, \text{hours}
$$

result.text: Ed Evans believed that employers do not understand the Welsh bac, but if they did, they would appreciate it. He thought that the Welsh bac helps students from less academic backgrounds by providing skills relevant to the civil engineering sector, and that employers should be engaged to support schools and colleges in delivering it.
